heading: Definitions
label: 3521 Definitions
notes: Amendments2011—Par. (1). substituted “section 105 (other than the Government Accountability Office)” for “section 105”.Pub. L. 112–74

Effective Date of 2011 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to voluntary separation incentive payments made during fiscal year 2012 or any succeeding fiscal year, see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Pub. L. 112–74section 1401(c) of Pub. L. 112–74section 4505 of Title 2

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–296, title XIII, § 1313(a)(4)116 Stat. 2294Nov. 25, 2002“This subsection [enacting this subchapter and provisions set out as notes under this section] shall take effect 60 days after the date of enactment of this Act [].”Nov. 25, 2002

Smithsonian Institution Employees, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–72, § 5117 Stat. 889Aug. 15, 2003“The Secretary of the Smithsonian Institution may establish a program for making voluntary separation incentive payments for employees of the Smithsonian Institution which is substantially similar to the program established under subchapter II of chapter 35 of title 5, United States Code (as added by section 1313(a) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 []).”Pub. L. 107–296

Judicial Branch Employees, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–296, title XIII, § 1313(a)(2)116 Stat. 2294Nov. 25, 2002“The Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts may, by regulation, establish a program substantially similar to the program established under paragraph (1) [enacting this subchapter] for individuals serving in the judicial branch.”

Continuation of Other Authority, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–296, title XIII, § 1313(a)(3)116 Stat. 2294Nov. 25, 2002“Any agency exercising any voluntary separation incentive authority in effect on the effective date of this subsection [see Effective Date note above] may continue to offer voluntary separation incentives consistent with that authority until that authority expires.”

Sense of Congress, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–296, title XIII, § 1313(c)116 Stat. 2296Nov. 25, 2002“It is the sense of Congress that the implementation of this section [enacting this subchapter, amending sections 8336 and 8414 of this title, enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and , and repealing provisions set out as notes under sections 8336 and 8414 of this title] is intended to reshape the Federal workforce and not downsize the Federal workforce.”section 8336 of this title

children.text: “agency” means an Executive agency as defined under section 105 (other than the Government Accountability Office); and

children.label: i
children.text: is serving under an appointment without time limitation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has been currently employed for a continuous period of at least 3 years; and

children.label: i
children.text: a reemployed annuitant under subchapter III of chapter 83 or 84 or another retirement system for employees of the Government;

children.label: ii
children.text: an employee having a disability on the basis of which such employee is or would be eligible for disability retirement under subchapter III of chapter 83 or 84 or another retirement system for employees of the Government;

children.label: iii
children.text: an employee who is in receipt of a decision notice of involuntary separation for misconduct or unacceptable performance;

children.label: iv
children.text: an employee who has previously received any voluntary separation incentive payment from the Federal Government under this subchapter or any other authority;

children.label: v
children.text: an employee covered by statutory reemployment rights who is on transfer employment with another organization; or

children.label: I
children.text: during the 36-month period preceding the date of separation of that employee, performed service for which a student loan repayment benefit was or is to be paid under section 5379;

children.label: II
children.text: during the 24-month period preceding the date of separation of that employee, performed service for which a recruitment or relocation bonus was or is to be paid under section 5753; or

children.label: III
children.text: during the 12-month period preceding the date of separation of that employee, performed service for which a retention bonus was or is to be paid under section 5754.
